i(pfd)a-1975-section-3(2)(b).text: Inheritance (Provision for Family and Dependants) Act 1975, Section 3(2)(b): the contribution made by the applicant to the welfare of the family of the deceased, including any contribution made by looking after the home or caring for the family.

mca-1973-section-25(2)(a).text: Matrimonial Causes Act 1973, Section 25(2)(a): the income, earning capacity, property and other financial resources which each of the parties to the marriage has or is likely to have in the foreseeable future, including in the case of earning capacity any increase in that capacity which it would in the opinion of the court be reasonable to expect a party to the marriage to take steps to acquire;
mca-1973-section-25(2)(a).bm25_text: matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25(a) income and earning capacity matters to which court is to have regard in deciding how to exercise its powers under ss. 23, 24 , 24a, 24b and 24e. matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25 2 a the income earning capacity property and other financial resources which each of the parties to the marriage has or is likely to have in the foreseeable future including in the case of earning capacity any increase in that capacity which it would in the opinion of the court be reasonable to expect a party to the marriage to take steps to acquire income earning capacity salary earnings wages financial resources property assets pension increase earning capacity reasonable steps to acquire foreseeable future resources
mca-1973-section-25(2)(a).section_title: Matters to which court is to have regard in deciding how to exercise its powers under ss. 23, 24 , 24A, 24B and 24E.

mca-1973-section-25(2)(b).text: Matrimonial Causes Act 1973, Section 25(2)(b): the financial needs, obligations and responsibilities which each of the parties to the marriage has or is likely to have in the foreseeable future;
mca-1973-section-25(2)(b).bm25_text: matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25(b) financial needs obligations matters to which court is to have regard in deciding how to exercise its powers under ss. 23, 24 , 24a, 24b and 24e. matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25 2 b the financial needs obligations and responsibilities which each of the parties to the marriage has or is likely to have in the foreseeable future needs financial needs obligations responsibilities outgoings expenses housing needs income needs financial obligations financial responsibilities

mca-1973-section-25(2)(c).text: Matrimonial Causes Act 1973, Section 25(2)(c): the standard of living enjoyed by the family before the breakdown of the marriage;

mca-1973-section-25(2)(d).text: Matrimonial Causes Act 1973, Section 25(2)(d): the age of each party to the marriage and the duration of the marriage;
mca-1973-section-25(2)(d).bm25_text: matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25(d) age and duration matters to which court is to have regard in deciding how to exercise its powers under ss. 23, 24 , 24a, 24b and 24e. matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25 2 d the age of each party to the marriage and the duration of the marriage age duration of marriage length of marriage how long married years of marriage age of parties age of each party marriage duration

mca-1973-section-25(2)(e).text: Matrimonial Causes Act 1973, Section 25(2)(e): any physical or mental disability of either of the parties to the marriage;
mca-1973-section-25(2)(e).bm25_text: matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25(e) disability matters to which court is to have regard in deciding how to exercise its powers under ss. 23, 24 , 24a, 24b and 24e. matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25 2 e any physical or mental disability of either of the parties to the marriage disability physical disability mental disability health ill health medical condition impairment disabled physical impairment mental impairment

mca-1973-section-25(2)(f).text: Matrimonial Causes Act 1973, Section 25(2)(f): the contributions which each of the parties has made or is likely in the foreseeable future to make to the welfare of the family, including any contribution by looking after the home or caring for the family;
mca-1973-section-25(2)(f).bm25_text: matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25(f) contributions matters to which court is to have regard in deciding how to exercise its powers under ss. 23, 24 , 24a, 24b and 24e. matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25 2 f the contributions which each of the parties has made or is likely in the foreseeable future to make to the welfare of the family including any contribution by looking after the home or caring for the family contributions homemaker stay-at-home parent welfare of family caring looking after home childcare housewife domestic contributions non-financial contributions raised children looking after the home caring for the family homemaker contributions stay at home

mca-1973-section-25(2)(g).text: Matrimonial Causes Act 1973, Section 25(2)(g): the conduct of each of the parties, if that conduct is such that it would in the opinion of the court be inequitable to disregard it;
mca-1973-section-25(2)(g).bm25_text: matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25(g) conduct matters to which court is to have regard in deciding how to exercise its powers under ss. 23, 24 , 24a, 24b and 24e. matrimonial causes act 1973 section 25 2 g the conduct of each of the parties if that conduct is such that it would in the opinion of the court be inequitable to disregard it conduct behaviour misconduct bad behaviour adultery affair inequitable to disregard gross and obvious fault bad behavior marital conduct conduct of parties
mca-1973-section-25(2)(g).section_title: Matters to which court is to have regard in deciding how to exercise its powers under ss. 23, 24 , 24A, 24B and 24E.

mca-1973-section-25(2)(h).text:
Matrimonial Causes Act 1973, Section 25(2)(h): in the case of proceedings for divorce or nullity of marriage, the value to each of the parties to the marriage of any benefit 



 ... which, by reason of the dissolution or annulment of the marriage, that party will lose the chance of acquiring.
